fields.description:
## Why
Gates must each read their own kind, and reopen must invalidate WITHOUT mutating records.
This slice flips the gates from the legacy single `signature` mirror to the kind-keyed
`attestations` map + validity-on-read, and removes the now-obsolete reopen mutation.
## What
Add `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind, *, repo_root=None) -> {"valid": bool,
"reason": str}` in the verification layer. It ASSUMES the attestation is already
HMAC-certified (the caller runs verify first) and adds ONLY the per-kind lifecycle/freshness
layer; it REUSES the existing plan-review material fingerprint
(`orchestrator.material_fingerprint`) as the single source of the material field set (no new
constant). Migrate the gates to read `attestations[kind]` via verify + compute_validity:
`claim_gate_check` -> `plan-review`; the close gate + completion child-closure check
(`completion.py`, currently `verify_signature(cid).get("verdict")`) -> `completion-verifier`.
PAIRED removal (no gap): REMOVE `retire_attested_pin` (signing.py) and its reopen call
(transition.py) IN THIS SAME slice, because compute_validity's reopen-awareness
(`signed_at >= last_reopened_at`, data recorded by 114f) now replaces it — they land
together so reopen-invalidation is never missing.
## Scope
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (`claim_gate_check`, `compute_validity`),
`src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` (close gate; remove retire_attested_pin reopen call),
`src/rebar/signing.py` (remove retire_attested_pin), `src/rebar/llm/completion.py`.
## Depends on
Requires 114f (attestations map, last_reopened_at) and cc1b (verify_signature kind=) merged.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind, *, repo_root=None)` returns `{"valid": bool, "reason": str}`, assumes certified, reuses the existing material fingerprint (no new constant).
- [ ] Claim gate verifies+validates `plan-review`; close + child-closure verify+validate `completion-verifier`, reading `attestations[kind]`.
- [ ] completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since `signed_at`.
- [ ] plan-review valid = existing freshness (material + filesystem code-drift) AND not-reopened-since.
- [ ] `retire_attested_pin` + its reopen call removed IN THIS SLICE, paired with compute_validity (no window with missing reopen-invalidation); no transition mutates any attestation record.
